chunks.text: Page 1 of 2 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, BENCH AT SUKKUR C.P.No. D- 1642 of 2021 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge. Hearing of Case (Priority) 1.For orders on office objection 2.For hearing of CMA 7942/2021 3.For hearing of main case Date of Hearing & Order: 07-04-2022 Haji Shamsuddin Rajper, Advocate for the Petitioners. Mr. Irfan Ahmed Panhwar, Advocate for the Respondents No.5&6. Mr. Ali Raza Balouch, Assistant Advocate General-Sindh. O R D E R Through this Petition, the Petitioners seek order of quo warranto against the Respondent No.5 on the ground that he does not qualify to hold the post of Headmaster, Government Murad High School, Gambat- Khairpur. 2. Learned Counsel for the Petitioners has argued that the Respondent No.5 lacks requisite qualification for being appointed as Headmaster; that he has been appointed on political basis and favoritism; that prior to this appointment, he was required to complete three years tenure of his earlier posting which has not been done, hence this Petition merits consideration and is liable to be allowed. 3. On the other hand, learned Assistant A.G and Counsel for the private Respondents have opposed this Petition. 4. We have heard all learned Counsel for the Parties and perused the record. Page 2 of 2 5. At the very outset, we have confronted the Petitioners’ Counsel as to the relief of quo warranto being sought on behalf of the Petitioners and the lack of requisite qualification of the Respondent No.5 and to that only response was that he was politically being appointed. To that, we may observe that this is no ground for exercising jurisdiction and discretion in respect of a quo warranto.

At the very outset, we have confronted the Petitioners’ Counsel as to the relief of quo warranto being sought on behalf of the Petitioners and the lack of requisite qualification of the Respondent No.5 and to that only response was that he was politically being appointed. To that, we may observe that this is no ground for exercising jurisdiction and discretion in respect of a quo warranto. Besides, no other justifiable ground has been made out. Therefore, this Petition does not merit any consideration and accordingly the same is hereby dismissed with pending application. JUDGE JUDGE Ahmad
